items.title: Ebola may be spreading faster than first thought, WHO doctor warns
items.summary: Hundreds of cases are suspected in central Africa but experts fear the actual number may be much higher.

items.title: Teen suspects fatally shoot three in suspected hate crime at San Diego mosque
items.summary: The alleged attackers were aged 17 and 18 and one left a note containing "generalised hate rhetoric", investigators say.

items.title: Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states
items.summary: The US president says he is holding off on an attack planned for Tuesday as "serious negotiations are now taking place".

items.title: Selling children to survive: Afghan fathers forced to make impossible choices
items.summary: In Afghanistan today, a staggering three in four people cannot meet their basic needs.

items.title: Two dead in Spain shooting, with babies reportedly among injured
items.summary: Police say a 25-year-old man is suspected of killing his parents and injuring four others in southern Spain.

items.title: Two bodies of missing Italians recovered from inside Maldives cave
items.summary: The two were among four divers located in the furthest chamber in the cave and their bodies were recovered by specialist divers.

items.title: What really holds China and Russia together
items.summary: The relationship endures despite a power imbalance, with both sides recognising that it is too important to fail.

items.title: Jackson Pollock painting sells for record $181m at auction
items.summary: The Abstract Expressionist's Number 7A, 1948 painting is now his most expensive artwork ever sold at auction.

items.title: Tasmanian government apologises over stolen body parts scandal
items.summary: An investigation found that 177 human specimens from dozens of bodies were secretly kept by a museum.

items.title: Starbucks Korea sacks CEO over controversial 'Tank Day' promotion
items.summary: Starbucks withdrew the campaign for its drink tumblers after many said it referenced a bloody crackdown.

items.title: Death toll from Israeli strikes on Lebanon passes 3,000, officials say
items.summary: It marks a grim milestone in the conflict between Israel and the Lebanese armed group Hezbollah since March, despite a nominal ceasefire.

items.title: Trump administration unveils $1.7bn fund to compensate his allies as he drops tax lawsuit
items.summary: The settlement will go towards individuals who allege they were subjected to unfair investigations.

items.title: Two men arrested over stunt at enclosure of famous monkey Punch
items.summary: Two US nationals have been arrested after one jumped the fence of the monkey, who went viral online, police say.

items.title: Shakira wins £50m tax refund from Spanish government
items.summary: The Colombian singer says the court ruling means the "narrative" that she was guilty now "crumbles".

items.title: Four killed in protests during Kenyan strikes over high fuel prices
items.summary: Thousands of commuters are stranded, with key roads in the capital Nairobi largely empty due to the protests over fuel costs.

items.title: Musk loses OpenAI court battle after jury finds he waited too long to sue
items.summary: Jurors spent weeks hearing about Musk's claim that Altman had "stolen a charity."

items.title: Bodies of missing Italian divers found in Maldives
items.summary: The bodies of all five Italian divers who died in the Maldives last week have now been found, officials tell the BBC.

items.title: Judge rules gun and writings are admissible in Luigi Mangione's New York murder trial
items.summary: Other items found with Mangione at a Pennsylvania McDonald's will not be allowed to be presented in court, the judge ruled.

items.title: Former Google CEO Eric Schmidt booed by graduates at mention of AI
items.summary: The reaction underscores a growing anxiety among students over AI's impact on jobs and their future careers.

items.title: Israeli forces board Gaza-bound flotilla near Cyprus, activists say
items.summary: The Global Sumud Flotilla says commandos have intercepted more than 40 boats trying to breach the Gaza maritime blockade since Tuesday.

items.title: Trump warns 'clock is ticking' for Iran as peace progress stalls
items.summary: On Monday Iran said it had responded to the latest US proposal and that exchanges with Washington were continuing.

items.title: Driver of crashed train tested positive for drugs, Thai police say
items.summary: The freight train collided with a bus in Bangkok, killing eight people and injuring dozens of others.

items.title: 'This may be the last time you hear my voice': Political executions surge in Iran since start of war
items.summary: Since the US and Israel attacked Iran on  28 February, the UN says it’s verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners.

items.title: Large-scale Ukrainian drone attack kills three in Moscow region, says Russia
items.summary: Ukraine's president says the strikes are a "justified" response to deadly Russian attacks on Ukrainian cities.

items.title: Escaped tiger shot by German police after attacking man
items.summary: The tiger escaped a facility owned by local trainer Carmen Zander, also known as Germany's "Tiger Queen", according to local media reports.

items.title: UAE reports strike near Abu Dhabi nuclear power plant
items.summary: The country is investigating the source of the strike, which it calls a "dangerous escalation".

items.title: 'Everest Man' and 'Mountain Queen' break own records scaling world's tallest peak
items.summary: Kami Rita Sherpa and Lakpa Sherpa summited Everest for the 32nd and 11th time, respectively.

items.title: The Republican who stood up to Trump is hoping for a big win against president
items.summary: Kentucky Congressman Thomas Massie, who has crossed Trump on federal spending and the Epstein files, faces Republican voters.

items.title: 'FedEx says your parcel has drugs': The scam that trapped an Indian comedian
items.summary: Ankita Shrivastav is among millions of Indians who fall prey to cybercrimes every year.

items.title: Trump told Taiwan not to 'go independent' - but does it want to?
items.summary: Taiwan independence is the reddest of red lines for China, which claims the island as its own.

items.title: What is Ebola and why is stopping this outbreak so difficult?
items.summary: An outbreak of Ebola in DR Congo involves a rare strain and is in an area affected by conflict.

items.title: Inside the 'kill-zone' on Ukraine's front line, where new weapons have transformed war
items.summary: Machines are increasingly replacing humans on the front line, but troops are still vital for defending territory.

items.title: From escaping child marriage 'to an old pervert' to becoming Sierra Leone's first lady
items.summary: The private world of one of Africa's most powerful political women is revealed in a BBC World Service exclusive.

items.title: Foreign tourists are falling out of love with Goa - here's why
items.summary: Foreign visitors to Goa have nearly halved from their pre-Covid peak even as domestic tourism is booming.

items.title: 'I sold it for over £1k': Swatch launch sparks 'chaotic' scenes
items.summary: Some shoppers have been trying to resell a new line of watches priced at £335 each for up to £16,000.

items.title: Moment two fighter jets collide mid-air at US air show
items.summary: Four crew members safely ejected from US Navy jets and are in a stable condition after the collision.

items.title: Eurovision winner Dara arrives to screaming fans in Bulgaria
items.summary: The 27-year-old's tune Bangaranga won Bulgaria its first ever title in the song contest.

items.title: Lights, camera, Bangaranga: Highlights from Eurovision
items.summary: Bulgarian singer Dara stormed to victory in the contest, while Ukraine broke the record for the longest note ever sung at Eurovision.

items.title: Watch: What did we learn from Trump's visit to China?
items.summary: President Trump has concluded a two-day visit to Beijing at a high-stakes summit with Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
